params.title: IOE MOCK TEST - I
params.description: Practice mock test for Institute of Engineering entrance examination. Test your knowledge across Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, and English.
params.course: Bachelor in Computer Engineering, Bachelor in Civil Engineering, Bachelor in Electronics And Communication Engineering, Bachelor in Electrical Engineering, Bachelor in Architecture Engineering
params.questions.0.questionText: A body of mass 5 kg is thrown vertically upward with a velocity of 10 m/s. What is the maximum height reached?
params.questions.0.explanation: Using v² = u² - 2gh, at max height v=0, so h = u²/2g = 100/20 = 5m
params.questions.1.questionText: The SI unit of electric current is:
params.questions.1.explanation: Ampere (A) is the SI unit of electric current.
params.questions.2.questionText: What is the derivative of sin(x)?
params.questions.2.explanation: The derivative of sin(x) with respect to x is cos(x).
params.questions.3.questionText: Which of the following is the chemical formula for water?
params.questions.3.explanation: Water has the chemical formula H2O (two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom).
params.questions.4.questionText: In a right triangle, if one angle is 90° and another is 30°, what is the third angle?
params.questions.4.explanation: Sum of angles in a triangle = 180°. So third angle = 180 - 90 - 30 = 60°.
params.questions.5.questionText: What is the value of acceleration due to gravity on the surface of the Earth?
params.questions.5.explanation: The standard value of acceleration due to gravity on Earth's surface is approximately 9.8 m/s².
params.questions.6.questionText: Which element has the atomic number 6?
params.questions.7.questionText: What is the integral of 2x dx?
params.questions.8.explanation: Ohm's law states V = IR, where V is voltage, I is current, and R is resistance.
params.questions.9.questionText: The passive voice of 'She writes a letter' is:
params.questions.9.options.0.text: A letter is written by her.
params.questions.9.options.1.text: A letter was written by her.
params.questions.9.options.2.text: A letter has been written by her.
params.questions.9.options.3.text: A letter will be written by her.
params.questions.9.explanation: Present simple active → Present simple passive: 'is + past participle'.
